title: Spironolactone: MedlinePlus Drug Information

What special precautions should I follow? 
Before taking spironolactone, tell your doctor and pharmacist if you are allergic to spironolactone; any other medications; or any of the ingredients in spironolactone tablets. Ask your pharmacist for a list of the ingredients. tell your doctor if you are taking eplerenone (Inspra). Your doctor may tell you not to take spironolactone if you are taking this medication. some medications should not be taken with spironolactone. Other medications may cause dosing changes or extra monitoring when taken with spironolactone. Make sure you have discussed any medications you are currently taking or plan to take before starting spironolactone with your doctor and pharmacist. Before starting, stopping, or changing any medications while taking spironolactone, please get the advice of your doctor or pharmacist. the following nonprescription products may interact with spironolactone: potassium supplements; nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDS) such as ibuprofen (Advil, Motrin, others) and naproxen (Aleve); acetylsalicylic acid. Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking spironolactone. Do not start any of these medications while taking spironolactone without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your do
